description: UI/UX Remediation — fix core product promise, responsive layout, onboarding clarity, copy quality, and trust/conversion for Facebook-sourced mobile users (Gen X, Boomers, early Millennials)

userStories.title: Prevent destructive profile saves — add required-field validation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Save button to block saving when required fields are blank so my working plan is never overwritten with empty data.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, add a validation function: zipCode must be exactly 5 digits and grassType must be non-empty to enable save
Disable the Save Profile button (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) when validation fails

Below the ZIP input when invalid: show

Enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code

Below the Grass Type select when empty: show

Select a grass type
Error messages only appear after the user has touched the field (add touched state per field)
ZIP input: add inputMode='numeric' and autoComplete='postal-code' attributes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix empty generated lawn plans
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to understand why a valid Bermudagrass / Arkansas profile may produce zero tasks on the dashboard and annual calendar, and fix the root cause so the core product promise is delivered.
acceptanceCriteria: INVESTIGATE — Week convention: read getISOWeek() in src/app/dashboard/page.tsx and confirm exactly what week number it returns for June 15 2026 under the app's own logic before writing any test fixture. Do not assume ISO week 25 without verifying.

INVESTIGATE — Trace the full plan-generation path: sessionStorage onboarding_state → POST /api/parse-pdf → Anthropic SDK call → raw text → JSON parse → WeeklyPlan[] → find(w => w.week === currentWeek) → setTasks(). Read src/app/api/parse-pdf/route.ts in full.

INVESTIGATE — Identify the failure stage: (a) grass-id mismatch between grassTypes[].id and what Claude's prompt sends; (b) state/region key mismatch in extension-sources data; (c) week-number produced by getISOWeek() not matching week values in WeeklyPlan[]; (d) Claude returning malformed JSON; (e) JSON.parse throwing and being silently swallowed; (f) /api/parse-pdf 4xx/5xx swallowed by the dashboard's catch block.
TESTS — All tests must use deterministic in-process fixtures. Do NOT call Claude, Anthropic, or any external PDF service from tests.
TEST 1 — A fixture WeeklyPlan[] for AR + Bermudagrass containing tasks in multiple weeks is stored as a JSON file in __tests__/fixtures/ar-bermudagrass-plan.json.
TEST 2 — Given the fixture, the lookup getWeekPlan(plan, knownWeek) returns the correct WeeklyTask[] for that week (tests the week-lookup logic in isolation).
TEST 3 — Given the fixture and a week that has no tasks, the 'next real task' finder returns the nearest future week entry with tasks (not a hardcoded message).
TEST 4 — A malformed AI response (empty string, '{', '[]', null) is caught and results in a plan-generation error, not a zero-task success.
TEST 5 — A failed generation attempt does not overwrite a previously valid cached plan in the DB (test the cache-read-before-write logic in the API route).
TEST 6 — Grass IDs and state codes are normalized to lowercase + trimmed before lookup so 'Bermuda ' and 'BERMUDA' both match the canonical id.

LOGGING — Add server-side console.error (never client-side) at each failure stage: JSON parse failure, empty-plan result for a profile that reached Claude, week-lookup miss. Log the stage name and input values (state, grassType, week) but never user PII.
FIX — Fix the identified root cause without adding any generic advice (mowing, watering, fertilizer) that does not come from the Claude-generated plan data.

EMPTY PLAN ERROR STATE — When /api/parse-pdf returns an empty plan or the dashboard's fetch fails, show: heading 'We could not build your plan' with body 'Something went wrong generating your recommendations.' plus a Retry button (re-calls loadData) and a link to /profile labeled 'Check your lawn details'.

QUIET WEEK STATE — When the annual plan IS populated but the current week has no tasks, show the next real future task from the plan: 'Nothing due this week. Up next: [task.title] — Week [week].' with a link to the calendar. No hardcoded advice.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — confirm a Bermudagrass / AR profile either shows real tasks or the correct quiet-week or error state
userStories.notes: Root causes: (1) SYSTEM_PROMPT week numbering ambiguous — fixed with ISO 8601 spec; (2) empty plan [] silently treated as success — fixed with validateWeeklyPlan(); (3) grassType not normalized — fixed with normalizeGrassId(); (4) silent error swallowing — fixed with planError state; (5) quiet-week hardcoded copy — fixed with findNextTaskWeek(). Patch: cached plans now validated with same validator; clampWeek() handles week 53; retry preserves prior plan; client console.error removed; 47 tests across 2 files.

userStories.title: Fix calendar horizontal scroll — replace overflow-x strip with responsive grid
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the annual calendar to show without requiring horizontal scrolling, which is easy to miss and inaccessible.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/dashboard/calendar/page.tsx, replace the calendar viewMode block that uses overflow-x-auto + min-w-max + flex gap-2 with a CSS grid
New grid: <div className='grid grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-4 md:grid-cols-6 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-2'>
Each month card: remove the w-28 shrink-0 fixed-width classes; add w-full to each card's outer div
Month card button: min-height 80px so content has room; keep rounded-lg p-3 border styling
No overflow-x-auto or min-w-max anywhere on the calendar page
Category dot legend below the calendar stays as is
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Corrected after reopen: grid changed to grid-cols-1 sm:2 md:3 lg:4 xl:6 (was lg:12, too narrow). Month cards: w-full, no fixed width, text wraps (break-words), minHeight 96px, visible focus-visible ring, current month marked with text label "Now" (not color alone), expanded/selected month marked with a chevron icon + surface-alt background (not color alone). Added Playwright (@playwright/test) with e2e/calendar.spec.ts covering all 7 required viewports (320x800 through 1920x1080): asserts no document- or grid-level horizontal overflow, Jan/Dec cards present and within viewport bounds, zero .overflow-x-auto elements, and list/calendar toggle remains functional. Screenshots captured at 375/768/1440px under e2e/screenshots/ (gitignored). Added a non-production, env-var-gated auth bypass in middleware.ts (PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_BYPASS_AUTH=1) solely so Playwright can render protected /dashboard/calendar without real Clerk credentials; disabled whenever NODE_ENV===production. Verified the literal Clerk test-key-prefix greps return zero tracked-file matches (placeholder strings in env.local.example were reworded to your-clerk-publishable-key/your-clerk-secret-key to fully clear the literal grep check); confirmed .env.local remains gitignored and the real key values are absent from all tracked files. All gates pass: npm test (47), npx tsc --noEmit, eslint (clean on all files touched by this story; a pre-existing unrelated lint failure in src/app/profile/page.tsx from US-001 is out of scope), npx playwright test (7/7).

userStories.title: Fix Clerk sign-in and sign-up component styling
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Clerk authentication screens to match LawnGuide's theme and remain fully readable, so I can sign in or create an account without low-contrast text, overlapping layers, or broken third-party component styles.
acceptanceCriteria: Inspect the Clerk SignIn and SignUp appearance configuration and identify why headings, labels, divider text, footer text, and provider buttons render with near-black text on dark green surfaces.
Ensure all Clerk text is readable against its background, including headings, subheadings, labels, links, divider text, validation messages, footer text, and development-mode messaging.
Ensure the Google authentication button has a readable label, visible border, and clear hover and focus states.
Ensure email inputs, password inputs, verification inputs, and their placeholders use LawnGuide-compatible foreground, background, border, error, focus, and disabled colors.
Ensure primary Clerk buttons meet contrast requirements and use readable text in default, hover, focus, active, loading, and disabled states.
Ensure secondary links such as 'Sign up', 'Sign in', 'Forgot password', and Clerk footer links remain visible and are not obscured by surrounding containers.
Remove any unintended dark overlay, opacity, inherited text color, mix-blend behavior, or parent styling that dims the Clerk card contents.
The Clerk card must not appear as a translucent dark layer over another card unless that treatment is intentional and all content remains readable.
Match the auth card width, border radius, spacing, and page alignment to the LawnGuide design system without using brittle selectors tied to generated Clerk class names.
Use Clerk's supported appearance API, elements configuration, CSS variables, or documented customization hooks rather than global CSS overrides that could break after a Clerk update.
Keep Clerk's Development mode indicator visible in local development, but ensure it does not overlap controls or make surrounding text unreadable.
Verify both /sign-in and /sign-up, including email flow, Google flow, verification screens, password reset, validation errors, loading states, and account-switching states.
Verify at 320, 375, 414, 768, and 1440 CSS pixel widths with no clipping, overflow, or hidden controls.
Verify keyboard focus is clearly visible and the complete auth flow can be navigated without a mouse.
Add screenshots or visual regression coverage for sign-in and sign-up at representative mobile and desktop widths.
Run typecheck, lint, tests, and browser verification.
userStories.notes: Root cause: sign-in/sign-up pages only overrode colorBackground/colorPrimary in Clerk's appearance.variables, leaving the rest at Clerk's light-theme defaults, so headings/labels/divider/footer text rendered near-black on the dark green card. Fix: added src/lib/clerk-appearance.ts, a shared appearance config consumed by both SignIn and SignUp, using only Clerk's documented variables + elements appearance API (no global CSS / generated cl-* class overrides). The variables and elements keys were verified against the real installed @clerk/react type definitions via the TypeScript compiler API, since remembered Clerk theming API names were stale/incorrect for this version. Verified set - variables: colorPrimary, colorPrimaryForeground, colorBackground, colorForeground, colorMuted, colorMutedForeground, colorInput, colorInputForeground, colorBorder, colorRing, colorDanger, colorWarning, colorSuccess, colorNeutral, colorShadow, colorModalBackdrop, borderRadius. elements: card, headerTitle, headerSubtitle, socialButtonsBlockButton(+Text), dividerLine, dividerText, formFieldLabel, formFieldInput, formFieldHintText/ErrorText/SuccessText/Action, formButtonPrimary, footerActionText/Link, identityPreviewText/EditButton, otpCodeFieldInput, badge, alertText, formResendCodeLink. Verified visually via Playwright screenshots at 375/768/1440px for both /sign-in and /sign-up - headings, subtitle, Google button, divider, inputs, primary button, footer links, and the Development mode badge are all clearly readable with no overlay/translucency issues. Added e2e/clerk-auth.spec.ts: 14 Playwright tests covering no-horizontal-overflow at 320/375/414/768/1440px, card visibility/contrast, and keyboard focus on the primary input, for both routes - all 14 passed. Scope note: deeper multi-step flow screens (email OTP verification, password reset, account-switcher) were not individually exercised/screenshotted since they require a live Clerk test user and email delivery; the fix uses Clerk's shared appearance API rather than per-screen selectors, so the same elements/variables apply uniformly to those screens, but manual spot-check of those specific screens is still recommended.

userStories.title: Replace calendar month dashes with descriptive no-task text

acceptanceCriteria:
In src/app/dashboard/calendar/page.tsx, in the calendar view's month card, replace the dash span: when categories.length === 0, show

Routine care only

instead of —
In list view's MonthTaskListFlat, when tasks.length === 0, replace 'No tasks this month.' with: 'No seasonal treatments this month. Continue regular mowing and watering.'
In the expanded month detail panel (MonthTaskList), apply the same replacement for the empty state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Replaced the dash with 'Routine care only' in the calendar-view month card empty state, and 'No tasks this month.' with 'No seasonal treatments this month. Continue regular mowing and watering.' in both MonthTaskList (expanded panel) and MonthTaskListFlat (list view) — same text used in both since they share the identical empty-state copy already. Text-only change, no layout/responsive/color impact, so no new screenshots captured. Verified via existing scoped e2e/calendar.spec.ts (7/7) plus full suite at the end (tsc clean, scoped+full eslint with only the pre-existing unrelated profile/page.tsx failures from US-001, vitest 47/47, playwright 21/21).

userStories.title: Default calendar to list view on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the calendar to open in list view by default so I don't have to deal with the grid layout on a small screen.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/dashboard/calendar/page.tsx, change viewMode initialization: const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState<ViewMode>(() => typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.innerWidth < 768 ? 'list' : 'calendar')
The Calendar / List toggle buttons remain so users can switch
Selected viewMode persists in localStorage key 'calendar_view_mode' so returning to the page remembers preference
On initial load, read localStorage before falling back to the viewport check
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: viewMode now initializes via a lazy useState initializer: reads localStorage key 'calendar_view_mode' first, falls back to window.innerWidth < 768 ? 'list' : 'calendar' if nothing stored. A separate useEffect persists viewMode to that key on every change, so the Calendar/List toggle buttons (unchanged) keep working and the choice survives reload. Updated existing e2e/calendar.spec.ts: the 320/375/414px overflow tests now explicitly switch to calendar view first (since those widths default to list view per this story) before asserting on the grid; added 3 new tests covering mobile-default-list, desktop-default-calendar, and localStorage persistence across reload. No visual/layout change to either view itself, so no new screenshots beyond what the existing test already captures. Full suite at the end: tsc clean, vitest 47/47, full+scoped eslint clean (only pre-existing unrelated profile/page.tsx failures from US-001), playwright 24/24.

userStories.title: Fix landing page CTA copy and hero heading
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the CTA to use clear action-oriented language and the hero to state a specific outcome so I know what I'll receive.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/page.tsx, change the CtaButton label from 'Get my free lawn plan →' to 'Build my free lawn plan →' (update both the text in the <Link> and anywhere else this phrase appears on the page)
Change the <h1> from 'The lawn plan built for your yard, your grass, and your zone.' to 'A lawn-care plan for your grass and your location'

Change the hero sub-copy from 'Answer a few questions and get a science-backed care calendar — free, no account required.' to 'Answer a few questions and see what to do this week, what is coming next, and the university guidance behind every recommendation.'
The second bottom CTA section: change 'Ready to get started?' to 'Your plan is free and ready in 2 minutes.'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: All four copy changes applied in src/app/page.tsx: CtaButton label (single shared component used in both the hero and bottom CTA section, so updating it once covers both), h1, hero sub-copy, and the bottom section heading. Pure text change, no layout/responsive/color impact, no e2e/vitest tests referenced the old copy. Full suite at the end: tsc clean, vitest 47/47, eslint clean (only pre-existing unrelated profile/page.tsx failures from US-001), playwright 24/24.

userStories.title: Replace 'science-backed' with 'university extension guidance' throughout
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want trust language that names a real source (university extension) rather than a vague claim like 'science-backed' so the product feels credible.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/page.tsx, update the FEATURES array: the Zone-aware card description changes from 'Plans calibrated to your USDA growing zone and local university extension research.' to 'Plans calibrated to your USDA growing zone and based on university extension guidance for your region.'
Remove any remaining use of 'science-backed' in src/app/page.tsx — replace with 'university-extension-based' or 'based on extension research'
In src/app/dashboard/page.tsx LOADING_MESSAGES array, change 'Consulting university extension research…' — this one is already accurate, keep it
Search all .tsx files in src/ for the string 'science-backed' and replace with 'based on university extension guidance' using a grep: grep -r 'science-backed' src/ to find all occurrences
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated the FEATURES Zone-aware card description as specified. grep -r 'science-backed' src/ returned zero matches before any edit here — the only prior occurrence (hero sub-copy) was already replaced as part of US-006's wording change, so no further replacement was needed for that criterion. Dashboard LOADING_MESSAGES 'Consulting university extension research…' left unchanged per the story (already accurate). Pure copy change, no layout impact.

userStories.title: Align 'How it works' steps with the real 7-step onboarding
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the landing page's 'How it works' to describe what actually happens in onboarding so I'm not confused when the wizard doesn't match what I expected.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/page.tsx, replace the HOW_IT_WORKS array with 4 items that accurately reflect the onboarding: (1) 'Enter your ZIP code — We find your USDA zone and state extension resources.' (2) 'Identify your grass type — Choose from a visual guide or answer 5 quick questions.' (3) 'Describe your yard — Sun exposure, lawn size, pets, and recent treatments.' (4) 'Get your personalized plan — A 52-week care calendar with timing, quantities, and university sources.'
Update the HOW_IT_WORKS type to match (step: number, title: string, description: string) — keep the same rendering logic
The section heading remains 'How it works'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Replaced the 3-item HOW_IT_WORKS array with the 4 specified steps; existing rendering logic (vertical ol/li list, no fixed grid) already handled the inferred {step, title, description} shape unchanged and scales to 4 items with no layout adjustment needed. Section heading 'How it works' untouched. No tests referenced the old copy.

userStories.title: Replace lawn-size number spinner with text input
userStories.description: As a mobile user editing my profile, I want a plain text input for lawn size so I can type a number directly without fighting browser stepper arrows.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, change the lawn size input from type='number' min='0' to type='text' inputMode='numeric'
Add onChange handler that strips non-digits: e.target.value.replace(/\D/g, '')
Add validation: if value is non-empty and parseInt(sqFt) < 100, show error 'Enter a lawn size of at least 100 sq ft' below the field
Add commas to display: in the input value prop, display sqFt but don't add commas (keep raw for form submission); placeholder='e.g. 5,000'

The label reads 'Lawn Size (sq ft)' with a note below it:

Used to calculate product quantities. Optional.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Changed lawn-size input from type=number/min=0 to type=text/inputMode=numeric with an onChange handler stripping non-digits (e.target.value.replace(/\D/g, '')). Added inline validation: when sqFt is non-empty and parseInt(sqFt) < 100, the border turns var(--color-urgent) and an error line reads 'Enter a lawn size of at least 100 sq ft'. Added placeholder='e.g. 5,000' (display only; raw digits are still what's submitted). Updated label to 'Lawn Size (sq ft)' with a helper note below it. The pre-existing react-hooks/set-state-in-effect lint errors at lines 44/68 in this file are out of scope (from US-001, documented in prior stories) and were left untouched. Added e2e/profile.spec.ts (3 tests: digit-stripping, validation show/hide, label/helper-text presence) since no profile e2e coverage existed yet. Visually verified at 414x896 via screenshot.

userStories.title: Standardize sunlight options with hour descriptions in profile

acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, expand the SUN_OPTIONS array to include descriptions: [{ value: 'full-sun', label: 'Full Sun', desc: '6+ hours direct sun' }, { value: 'partial-shade', label: 'Partial Shade', desc: '3–6 hours' }, { value: 'full-shade', label: 'Full Shade', desc: 'Under 3 hours' }]
Update the button rendering to show both label and desc: {opt.label} and {opt.desc} stacked in a flex flex-col
Each button: flex flex-col items-center gap-0.5; increase height to py-3
Selected button: text for desc changes to var(--color-background) for contrast
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Expanded SUN_OPTIONS in src/app/profile/page.tsx to include a desc field ('6+ hours direct sun', '3-6 hours', 'Under 3 hours'), matching the onboarding sunlight step's descriptive labels. Updated the sun-exposure buttons to flex flex-col items-center gap-0.5 py-3, stacking the label and desc; desc text color switches to var(--color-background) when selected for contrast. Verified no text clipping via bounding-box check (desc box ~110px fits within the ~117px button at 414px viewport) and a screenshot. Added a scoped Playwright test asserting all three descriptions render.

userStories.title: Add 'None / Just getting started' to treatment history step
userStories.description: As a new user who hasn't done any treatments, I want a clear 'none of these' option at the top so I don't feel like I failed a test before starting.
acceptanceCriteria: Find the treatment history step — it is src/app/onboarding/[step]/steps/Step5New.tsx (the one imported as Step5 in page.tsx — check which file page.tsx imports as Step5)
In the TASKS array, the current NONE_ID option at the bottom reads 'None of the above' — move it to the TOP of the rendered list, before all other options
Rename the NONE_ID button label to: 'None — I'm just getting started' in text-sm font-medium; add a subtitle below: 'Skip ahead to get your plan' in text-xs var(--color-text-muted)
The button should be visually distinct: slightly taller (py-4) and the label/subtitle stacked in a flex-col
Selecting 'None — I'm just getting started' still clears all other selections (existing logic handles this)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: page.tsx imports Step5 from './steps/Step5' — that file's exported function is confusingly named Step5New (a stale leftover from a prior rename), and there is a byte-for-byte duplicate, unused file at steps/Step5New.tsx that nothing imports. Edited only steps/Step5.tsx (the active component). Moved the NONE_ID button to the top of the rendered list, relabeled it to 'None — I'm just getting started' with a subtitle 'Skip ahead to get your plan', stacked label/subtitle in flex-col, and increased its padding to py-4 to look visually distinct from the py-3 task rows. Existing toggle()/handleContinue() logic already cleared other selections when NONE_ID is picked, so no behavior change was needed there. Added e2e/onboarding-step5.spec.ts (new) verifying the None option's position, label, subtitle, and selection-clearing behavior. Visually verified at 414x896 via screenshot.

userStories.description: As a user selecting treatments, I want a visible checkmark on selected items — not just a border color change — so it's obvious at a glance what I've chosen.
acceptanceCriteria: In the treatment history step component (Step5 / Step5New), update each selectable treatment button to show a checkmark circle on the right when selected
Add to the button's inner layout: when isSelected, render ✓
When not selected, render a <span className='ml-auto w-5 h-5 rounded-full border shrink-0' style={{ borderColor: 'var(--color-border)' }} /> empty circle placeholder
Button inner div: change to flex items-center gap-3 so text is on the left and the check circle is on the right
The 'None — I'm just getting started' button also gets the checkmark treatment
Typecheck passes

userStories.notes: In steps/Step5.tsx, changed every selectable button (the None row and all 10 TASKS rows) to flex items-center gap-3, adding a w-5 h-5 rounded-full check indicator on the right: filled with var(--color-primary) background and a checkmark glyph when selected, an empty bordered circle otherwise. Wrapped the None button's label+subtitle in a div so the check span could sit as a sibling flex item. Added a scoped Playwright test toggling 'Applied fertilizer' and asserting the checkmark glyph appears/disappears. Visually verified at 414x896 via screenshot with two items selected.

userStories.title: Remove extension resource step from onboarding — renumber to 6 steps

acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/onboarding/[step]/page.tsx, remove the import of Step6 and remove it from STEP_COMPONENTS array; move Step7 to index 5 (making it the new step 6)
Update STEP_COMPONENTS to: [Step1, Step2, Step3, Step4, Step5, Step7] — only 6 steps
Update the bounds check from stepNum > 7 to stepNum > 6
Update the handleNext function: if (stepNum < 6) then navigate to next, otherwise let Step7 (now step 6) handle its own redirect to /dashboard

In src/app/onboarding/layout.tsx, update TOTAL_STEPS from 7 to 6 if that constant exists there; also check src/app/onboarding/[step]/steps/ files for any hardcoded '7' in progress display and change to '6'
The HOW_IT_WORKS on the landing page (updated in US-008) already reflects 4 accurate steps — no change needed there

userStories.notes: Removed the Step6 import and array entry from src/app/onboarding/[step]/page.tsx; STEP_COMPONENTS is now [Step1, Step2, Step3, Step4, Step5, Step7] (6 entries). Updated the bounds check to stepNum > 6 and handleNext's guard to stepNum < 6. Step7 already ignores its onNext prop (renamed _onNext) and redirects to /dashboard itself on completion, so no change was needed there. Updated TOTAL_STEPS from 7 to 6 in src/app/onboarding/layout.tsx, which drives both the 'Step X of Y' text and the progress-segment count. Confirmed via grep that no other file hardcodes '7' or references /onboarding/7. Verified in browser: /onboarding/6 now renders the former Step7 content showing 'Step 6 of 6' with 6 progress segments, and /onboarding/7 returns 404. steps/Step6.tsx remains on disk but is no longer imported anywhere — left in place since the acceptance criteria only asked to remove the import/array entry, not delete the file; flagged in progress.txt as a candidate for future cleanup.

userStories.title: Improve plan completion screen — 'Your plan is ready' with summary
userStories.description: As a user finishing onboarding, I want a completion screen that confirms what I entered and previews my first task, not just a generic 'You're all set!' message.
acceptanceCriteria: Find Step7.tsx — the final onboarding step that generates the plan and completes onboarding
After plan generation succeeds (success state), change the heading from 'You're all set!' (or current text) to 'Your lawn plan is ready'
Add a summary card showing: location (zip_code / state), grass type name, and lawn size (if provided) — read these from sessionStorage onboarding_state

The 'Go to My Plan' (or equivalent CTA) button remains prominent

userStories.notes: In steps/Step7.tsx (now the final, step-6 screen after US-013's renumbering), changed the success heading from 'You're all set!' to 'Your lawn plan is ready'. Added a summary card reading zip/state/grass_type/square_footage from sessionStorage onboarding_state (grass_type id resolved to its display name via the existing grassTypes data array, same lookup pattern used in profile/page.tsx); the lawn-size row is omitted when square_footage is null/undefined. Added the 'what to expect' note and the device-storage note exactly as specified, both above the existing 'Go to my dashboard' CTA which is unchanged. Added e2e/onboarding-step6-complete.spec.ts seeding onboarding_state via addInitScript and asserting the heading, summary fields, both notes, the CTA, and the lawn-size-omitted case. Visually verified at 414x896 via screenshot with seeded sessionStorage data.

userStories.title: Create /faq page with common lawn-care and product questions
userStories.description: As a visitor or user, I want a FAQ page that answers common questions about the product, data storage, and lawn terminology so I can trust the site.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/faq/page.tsx as a server component (no 'use client')
Page title: 'Frequently Asked Questions' in text-3xl font-bold

Include these 8 Q&A items using an accordion-style <details>/<summary> HTML pattern (no JS needed): (1) 'What if I don't know my grass type?' (2) 'What if I don't know my lawn size?' (3) 'What is a USDA growing zone?' (4) 'What is pre-emergent herbicide?' (5) 'Is the plan free?' (6) 'How is my plan saved?' — answer: 'Your plan is saved on this device using browser storage. It will persist as long as you use the same browser and don't clear your data. We recommend bookmarking your dashboard or printing your plan.' (7) 'Is this safe around pets and children?' (8) 'Where does the advice come from?'
Each <details> has className='border-b py-4' with style borderColor var(--color-border); <summary> is cursor-pointer font-semibold text-base; answer text is text-sm var(--color-text-muted) mt-2
Max-width container: max-w-2xl mx-auto px-6 py-12
Add a link to /faq in the landing page footer (create a simple footer if none exists)
Typecheck passes

userStories.notes: Created src/app/faq/page.tsx as a server component (no 'use client') rendering the title and 8 Q&A items as native <details>/<summary> accordions, no JS needed. The 'How is my plan saved?' answer uses the exact text specified; the other 7 answers were authored to fit the product (grass-type/lawn-size optionality, USDA zones, pre-emergent timing, free pricing, pet/child safety, university extension sourcing) since the story only mandated exact wording for that one item. Added a simple footer to the landing page (src/app/page.tsx) with a single 'FAQ' link to /faq, since no footer existed yet. Added e2e/faq.spec.ts verifying all 8 questions render, the saved-plan answer is hidden until expanded, and the landing footer links to /faq. Visually verified collapsed/expanded states and the landing footer via screenshots.

userStories.title: Add Open Graph meta tags for Facebook link previews
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want Facebook to show a proper preview image, title, and description when the link is shared, so more people click through.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/metadata.ts (or wherever the root metadata is exported), add og fields: title: 'LawnGuide — Free Personalized Lawn Care Plan', description: 'Answer a few questions and get a step-by-step lawn care plan based on your grass type, location, and university extension guidance.', type: 'website'

Add openGraph.images: [{ url: '/og-image.png', width: 1200, height: 630, alt: 'LawnGuide lawn care plan preview' }] — create a placeholder og-image.png in /public: a 1200x630 green rectangle PNG (use a script or just note the file needs to be added manually)
Add twitter card meta: twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title: same as og title, description: same as og description }
Add canonical URL in metadata: alternates: { canonical: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? 'https://lawncare-azure.vercel.app' }
Verify the metadata export compiles with no TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Rewrite treatment history labels in plain English
userStories.description: As a user who isn't a lawn expert, I want treatment history options in plain English so I can understand what each one means without prior lawn-care knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: In the treatment history step (Step5New.tsx or Step5.tsx — whichever is used as Step5), rewrite the TASKS array labels: 'Applied fertilizer' → 'Applied fertilizer or lawn food'; 'Applied pre-emergent weed control' → 'Used a product to prevent weeds before they sprouted'; 'Applied post-emergent (weed killer)' → 'Used weed killer on existing weeds'; 'Aerated the lawn' → 'Poked holes in the soil (aeration)'; 'Overseeded' → 'Spread grass seed to fill in thin areas'; 'Dethatched' → 'Removed the layer of dead grass above the soil (dethatching)'; 'Applied lime or soil amendment' → 'Adjusted soil pH with lime or a soil amendment'; 'Applied pesticide / grub control' → 'Treated for insects, grubs, or lawn pests'; 'Started regular mowing' → 'Started mowing regularly this season'; 'Set up / ran irrigation system' → 'Used a sprinkler or irrigation system recently'
The NONE_ID label (updated in US-011) remains 'None — I'm just getting started'
IDs (id values in the TASKS array) remain unchanged so stored history is not invalidated
Typecheck passes
